title: Count the Bits
description: Given a value k and a number of bits b, calculate the total number of 1-bits in the binary representations of all multiples of k that are between 0 and 2b-1 (inclusive).
input:
Each input will consist of a single test case. Note that your program may be run multiple times on different inputs.

Each test case will consist of a single line containing two space-separated integers k (1 ≤ k ≤ 1,000) and b (1 ≤ b ≤ 128), where k and b are as described above.
output: Output a single integer, which is the total number of 1-bits in the binary representations of all multiples of k that are between 0 and 2b-1 (inclusive). Since this number may be very large, output it modulo 109+9.
hint:
Consider the second sample: k=10 and b=5.

25-1 = 31. All the multiples of 10 between 0 and 31 are: 10, 20 and 30.

  • 10 = 01010b (2 1-bits)
  • 20 = 10100b (2 1-bits)
  • 30 = 11110b (4 1-bits)

That’s a total of 2+2+4=8 1-bits.
source: ICPC, Regionals, North America, Southeast USA Regional, 2018 Southeast USA Regional Programming Contest, Division 1, ICPC, Regionals, North America, Pacific Northwest Regional, 2018 Pacific Northwest Region Programming Contest, Division 1
